summary: analysis-chart ships an install-time Windows DLL dropper with scheduled-task persistence
details: On `npm install`, the `scripts.install` hook in package.json runs `node lib/chart-loader.js`, which downloads an encrypted payload from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc (chart-loader.js line 11), XOR-decrypts it with single-byte key 0x42 (line 12 + lines 48-50), base64-decodes the result, and validates a PE 'MZ' magic header (line 116) before writing the bytes to a random-named.dll under %APPDATA%\Roaming\Microsoft\Windows. The dropped DLL is executed via rundll32 and persisted by registering a scheduled task named 'WindowsUpdateService' to run on logon (line 57: `schtasks /create /tn "WindowsUpdateService" /tr "rundll32.exe..." /sc onlogon`). The script then deletes the package folder and removes its own entry from the parent package.json to hide traces. The package's `index.js` exports only innocuous statistical helpers (stats/outliers/trend/correlation) as a decoy, while all malicious behavior runs from the lifecycle hook. Installing this package on Windows results in remote code execution and persistent compromise of the installer's machine.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
